'''Gareth Raynor''' (born 24 February 1978) is an English former professional [[rugby union]] and [[rugby league]] footballer who last played for the [[London Skolars]] in [[RFL League 1|Championship 1]].<ref name="loverugbyleague.com">{{cite web| url = http://www.loverugbyleague.com/news_12749-skolars-sign-raynor.html| url-status = dead| archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20130811182757/http://www.loverugbyleague.com/news_12749-skolars-sign-raynor.html| archive-date = 2013-08-11| title = Skolars sign Raynor}}</ref> He is a former Great Britain international who previously played for [[Hull FC]], the [[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders]], the [[Leeds Rhinos]], and the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> Plus a spell with [[Aviva Premiership]] side, [[Leicester Tigers]].

==Background==
Raynor was born in [[Leeds]], [[West Yorkshire]], England. He started his career in [[rugby union]] as a centre. He started out at Pontefract RU and later moved onto Castleford Panthers before [[Leeds Carnegie|Leeds Tykes]] stumbled upon his performances and took a keen interest. It was not long after that he moved to the Tykes in 2000. He made 8 appearances (4 as substitute) for the Tykes in their Division One season.

Raynor left the Tykes in the summer and switched codes to play [[Rugby league]] for big next door club, [[Leeds Rhinos]].

==Professional playing career==
===2000s===
Gareth transferred to Leeds in the summer of 2000 and made three off the bench performances for the Rhinos. With little game time Gareth moved to Hull F.C. on a two-year contract. Injury to established winger [[Chris Smith (rugby league, born 1975)|Chris Smith]] led to more starts than expected for Raynor and he soon became a fans' favourite. After the 2002 Super League season, he returned to rugby union and played for the [[Leicester Tigers]]. He made four appearances in the 2002–03 season for the Leicester Tigers<ref name="Raynor rejoins Hull">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/england/leicester|title=Raynor rejoins Hull|publisher=BBC – Leicester|date=2003-06-23|access-date=2007-06-14}}</ref> with two from the substitute bench before leaving to return to rugby league with [[Hull F.C.]] midway through the 2003's [[Super League VIII]].<ref name="Gareth Raynor Profile">{{cite web|url=http://www.leicestertigers.com/squad/players.php?player=7360&includeref=dynamic|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160303184131/http://www.leicestertigers.com/squad/players.php?player=7360&includeref=dynamic|url-status=dead|archive-date=2016-03-03|title=Gareth Raynor Profile|publisher=Leicester Tigers|date=2003-06-23|access-date=2009-05-09}}</ref>

Raynor was a regular at [[Hull FC]] after a successful season on the wing with 18 tries in all competitions at the end of 2005. Raynor played for Hull at centre in the [[2005 Challenge Cup]] final, scoring a try in their victory against the [[Leeds Rhinos]]. Hull reached the [[2006 Super League Grand final]] to be contested against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], and Raynor played on the wing in his side's 4–26 loss.<ref>{{cite news|title=St Helens 26-4 Hull FC|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/6035553.stm|access-date=18 August 2013|work=[[BBC News]]|date=14 October 2006}}</ref> He became a [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] international, starring in the [[2006 Rugby League Tri-Nations|2006]] [[Rugby League Tri-Nations|Tri Nations]].
[[File:Gaz Raynor.jpg|200px|thumb|left|Gareth Raynor in action for Hull FC]]
He was forced to rule himself out of contention for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#England|England training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]] through injury.<ref name="Winger Raynor to miss World Cup">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7629764.stm|title=Winger Raynor to miss World Cup|publisher=BBC|date=2008-09-22|access-date=2008-10-03}}</ref>

===2010s===
For 2010's [[Super League XV]], Raynor signed for [[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders]] on a short-term deal, pending the results of his upcoming court case.<ref name="skysports">{{cite news| url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_5800962,00.html| publisher=Sky Sports| title=Hull confirm Raynor exit| date=2009-12-26}}</ref><ref name="Times">{{cite news| url=http://www.timesonline.co.uk/tol/sport/rugby_league/article6993117.ece| work=The Times| location=London| title=Crusaders make Gareth Raynor ninth new signing| first=Christopher| last=Irvine| date=2010-01-19| access-date=2010-05-03}}{{dead link|date=September 2024|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}}</ref> During the opening match of 2010's Super League XV against [[Leeds Rhinos]], Raynor scored Crusaders first try of the season and only try of the match, going on to lose 34–6.

In May 2010, Raynor was jailed for 15 months for his part in a fake ink cartridge and computer games scam, and for breaching a previously suspended sentence relating to an assault.<ref name="jail">{{cite news|title=Rugby player Gareth Raynor jailed for internet scam|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/1/hi/england/humber/8688359.stm|publisher=BBC|access-date=18 May 2010| date=2010-05-17}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Gareth Raynor sentenced to 15 months' jail for ink cartridge scam |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2010/may/18/gareth-raynor-jail-counterfeit-scam |website=The Guardian |access-date=21 March 2025 |date=18 May 2010}}</ref> He was granted an early release after serving seven months of his sentence.<ref>{{cite web |title=Bradford Bulls recruit Raynor 'a changed man' after seven months in prison |url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/8785511.bradford-bulls-recruit-raynor-a-changed-man-after-seven-months-in-prison/ |website=Bradford Telegraph and Argus |access-date=21 March 2025 |date=12 January 2011}}</ref>

On 16 September 2010 it was confirmed that he had signed a 1-year deal with the Bradford Bulls.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/news/4960/RAYNOR%20WINGS%20IN%20FOR%20BULLS|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120323000853/http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/news/4960/RAYNOR%20WINGS%20IN%20FOR%20BULLS|url-status=dead|archive-date=2012-03-23|title = Raynor wings in for Bulls}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/bradford/9004067.stm|title=Bradford Bulls sign winger Gareth Raynor|date=2010-09-16|work=BBC Sport|publisher=BBC|access-date=2010-10-13}}</ref> Raynor featured in three of the four pre-season games. He played against [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]], [[Dewsbury Rams]] and [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]]. He scored a try against Halifax.

Gareth was featured from Round 1 ([[Leeds Rhinos]]) to Round 5 ([[Huddersfield Giants]]). He picked up an injury then returned in Round 10 ([[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]]) through to Round 12 ([[Catalans Dragons]]). Raynor's next game would be Round 14 ([[Hull Kingston Rovers]]), he was suspended for the next two games. He then played in Round 17 ([[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]). Gareth then played in seven consecutive games from Round 19 (Leeds Rhinos) until Round 25 ([[Wigan Warriors]]). Raynor was then featured in Round 27 ([[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]]). He also appeared in the [[Challenge Cup]] games against [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] and Wigan Warriors during this match he was sent off for punching [[Sam Tomkins]]. He scored tries against Huddersfield Giants (1 try), Hull Kingston Rovers (1 try), Catalans Dragons (1 try), [[Castleford Tigers]] (1 try) and Halifax (2 tries). He signed a 1-year extension with the Bradford Bulls.

His contract with Bradford was terminated in September 2011 for "a severe breach of club discipline".<ref>{{cite web |title=Gareth Raynor leaves Bradford Bulls for breach of club rules |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/15015363 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=21 March 2025 |date=22 September 2011}}</ref>

He signed for the Featherstone Rovers for the 2012 season, but he only played 4-matches, scoring 3-tries.

After 22 July 2013 he signed a year-long contract with the [[London Skolars]] in the [[RFL League 1|Kingstone Press Championship 1]]<ref name="loverugbyleague.com"/> which proved as quite a shock to all the rugby league admirers. He provided a try 2 minutes into his début for the London Skolars against [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]].
